q: Optics is the branch of physics that studies only the reflection of light.
A laser is an acronym for Light Amplification by Spontaneous Emission of Radiation.
Laser light is characterized by a narrow beam of intense, monochromatic, coherent light.
Laser light typically falls only within the visible spectrum of electromagnetic radiation.
Lasers produce light through spontaneous emission of photons from excited atoms.
The power in a continuous beam of commercial lasers can range up to a few megawatts.
Fiber lasers are one of the five main types of lasers.
A laser contains two essential elements: laser medium and optical cavity.
The laser medium (gain medium) is the material that absorbs the laser beam.
The laser medium can be a gas, liquid, crystalline solid or semiconductor crystal.
Pumping is the process where the power supply delivers energy to the laser medium to excite it to emit light.
The optical cavity (or resonator) of a laser is designed to absorb laser radiation.
Photons are absorbed in the optical cavity, decreasing the probability of stimulated emission.
The optical cavity consists of two highly reflective mirrors.
Both mirrors in a laser's optical cavity have almost 100% reflectivity.
Stimulated emission is the only essential process required to initiate laser action.
Atoms undergoing stimulated emission radiate photons of different frequencies and directions.
For stimulated emission to occur, a population inversion is needed.
Population inversion means the population of the lower level of a transition would have to be higher than that of the upper level.
If the lower level is not depopulated and the upper level is not populated, the population inversion will end.
A key characteristic of laser light is its broad beam divergence.
Laser emission is incoherent, meaning its light waves are out of phase with one another.
Laser light is monochromatic, meaning it contains a wide range of wavelengths.
A modest laser power cannot produce a high intensity due to the wide divergence of the beam.
One of the unique properties of a laser beam is coherence.
Lasers are used in medical applications such as bloodless surgery and cancer diagnosis.
Laser light is used in optical fibre communications for short-distance data transmission only.
Laser range finders are used to determine the distance to an object.
High power lasers are used in industries primarily for heating objects.
Lasers are used in shops and business establishments to collect information from the bar code printed on the product.
Lasers are used in the semiconductor industries for photolithography, in computer printers and to store and retrieve information in compact disc (CD).
